title: Add a /healthz readiness detail + document it — docs-coupling, path-scope & trajectory must stay content-general
intent: Add a richer /healthz readiness payload (uptime + store size) to the URL-shortener and document it.
note: Stresses code-review/doc-coupling, dev-fleet/path-scope, and dev-fleet/trajectory on a DIFFERENT feature than S1 (a /healthz refactor, not rate limiting). Proves those gates enforce the content-general rule 'arch changed ⇒ docs+test expected; stay in your lane', with no rate-limit-specific knowledge. Uses the doc-coupling / path-scope / trajectory drivers; no request oracle.
